message:
fix(evidence): address review bugs in timeline/chart components

Bug fixes from fresh-eyes review:

EvidenceTimeline.tsx:
- Add `onCloseDetail` prop for controlled mode - X button now works when
  parent provides selection callbacks
- Improve controlled mode detection logic

ConfidenceChart.tsx:
- Fix tooltip "Initial" label not showing on first point - was incorrectly
  wrapped in `{point.entry && ...}` conditional which excluded null entries
- Add AnimatePresence wrapper for tooltip exit animations
- Guard against undefined points[hoveredIndex] in tooltip render
- Rename ResizeObserver callback param from `entries` to `observerEntries`
  to avoid shadowing component prop

ConfidenceChart.test.tsx:
- Add AnimatePresence to framer-motion mock

Also includes additional objection type classification tests.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-bzl","title":"Test: Jargon component unit tests","description":"# Jargon Component Tests

## Goal
Write comprehensive unit tests for the Jargon component following project's NO MOCKS policy.

## Test Categories

### 1. Rendering Tests
- Known term renders with dotted underline
- Unknown term renders children without styling
- Custom children override default term display
- className prop is applied

### 2. Tooltip Behavior (desktop mock)
- Hover triggers tooltip visibility
- Mouse leave hides tooltip (after delay)
- Focus triggers tooltip visibility
- Blur hides tooltip (after delay)
- Tooltip content includes term, short, analogy

### 3. Sheet Behavior (mobile mock)
- Click/tap opens sheet
- Backdrop click closes sheet
- X button closes sheet
- Sheet content includes full term details

### 4. Accessibility
- Button has proper aria-label
- aria-expanded reflects state
- Focus management is correct

### 5. Edge Cases
- Term key normalization (spaces → hyphens, lowercase)
- Portal renders to document.body
- Cleanup on unmount (no memory leaks)

## Test Setup

```typescript
// Use real dictionary data
import { jargonDictionary, getJargon } from "@/lib/jargon";

// Mock matchMedia for mobile detection
beforeEach(() => {
  Object.defineProperty(window, 'matchMedia', {
    value: vi.fn().mockImplementation(query => ({
      matches: query === '(max-width: 768px)' ? isMobile : false,
      addEventListener: vi.fn(),
      removeEventListener: vi.fn(),
    })),
  });
});
```

## Files
- `apps/web/src/components/__tests__/jargon.test.tsx`

9. Invalid/malformed inputs

 {"id":"brenner_bot-chnx","title":"E2E Tests: Fix Playwright module loading error","description":"The E2E tests are failing to load with error: 'Playwright Test did not expect test.describe() to be called here.'

## Error Details
All E2E tests fail during test discovery with this error. The issue persists across all spec files including accessibility.spec.ts which doesn't use any new Agent Mail features.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
The agent-mail-seeder.ts imports from src/test-utils/agent-mail-test-server.ts. When test-fixtures.ts (used by all E2E tests) is loaded, even with lazy imports, the type re-export still causes module resolution at config time.

## Possible Fixes
1. Move the SessionConfig type inline in test-fixtures.ts to avoid any imports from agent-mail-seeder
2. Remove the testSession fixture from the main test object and have tests import it separately
3. Use dynamic import() in the fixture instead of type exports
4. Move agent-mail-seeder to a separate fixture file that's only loaded by tests that need it

## Related Commits
- 0e02609: feat(e2e): add Agent Mail integration tests with test server seeding

## Blocking
- brenner_bot-59rs: E2E Tests Full Session Lifecycle","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-01T21:25:27.216534766-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-02T08:41:41.029817089-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-02T08:41:41.029817089-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed: E2E tests now run successfully (770 passed). The lazy import pattern in test-fixtures.ts is working correctly."}
